$comment: CallSafe WebRTC Signaling Protocol — CANONICAL SPECIFICATION. This file is the single source of truth. index.ts (TypeScript) and Protocol.kt (Kotlin) are GENERATED from this file — do not edit them by hand. Normative prose (transport, auth, routing, flows) lives in README.md alongside this file.

transport.framing: Every protocol message is a single WebSocket text frame containing one JSON object with a required string field "type"; all other payload fields are flat siblings of "type".

heartbeat.description: Clients send {"type":"ping"} every clientPingIntervalMs; the server replies {"type":"pong"} immediately. The server closes any connection that has produced no frames for serverIdleCloseMs.

claims.device_id: string — MUST equal the deviceId sent in device:connect (mismatch => error device_mismatch)
claims.business_id: string — the business this connection belongs to (for customers: the business being called, resolved from the widget handle at token issuance)

acquisition.business: Authenticated dashboard/mobile session exchanges its login session for a socket token over HTTPS (e.g. GET /api/socket-token).
acquisition.customer: The embed widget fetches a short-lived guest token scoped to the business handle over HTTPS (e.g. GET /api/v1/guest-token?handle=<handle>). No login required; issuance is rate-limited per IP.

rules: device:connect MUST be the first protocol message on a connection (ping is also permitted pre-auth).
Any other message before successful device:connect => error not_authenticated.
The server derives deviceId, businessId and role from the verified token, never from later message payloads.

ringing_timeout.description: Max time a call may stay in ringing before the server transitions it to timeout and notifies caller and ringing devices with call:timeout (phase=ringing).

connecting_timeout.description: Max time a call may stay in connecting before the server transitions it to timeout and notifies both participants with call:timeout (phase=connecting).

escalation_timeout.description: Max time in escalation_pending; on expiry the server reverts to connected and sends escalation:rejected (reason=timeout) to the requester.

reconnect_grace.description: When a participant's socket drops while the call is in connecting/connected/escalation_pending, the server holds the call for this long awaiting call:reconnect; on expiry the call transitions to failed (reason=peer_disconnected) and the remaining participant receives call:failed.

terminal_retention.description: A call session is retained at least this long after reaching a terminal state; call-scoped messages arriving during retention => error invalid_state, after cleanup => error call_not_found.

MediaCapabilities.description: What media tracks a peer can send and receive.

SessionDescription.description: Mirrors the W3C RTCSessionDescriptionInit; clients pass their native object through unchanged. The server relays it verbatim.

type.description: one of: offer | answer | pranswer | rollback

IceCandidate.description: Mirrors the W3C RTCIceCandidateInit. An empty-string candidate signals end-of-candidates. The server relays it verbatim.

ping.description: Application-level heartbeat. Server replies with pong.

error.audience: the connection whose message caused the error
error.description: Reports a rejected message or server-side failure. The offending message was NOT processed.

message.description: human-readable detail; not for programmatic use

relatedType.description: the type of the message that caused this error

server:shutdown.description: Server is shutting down; clients should reconnect (with backoff) after the socket closes.

gracePeriod.description: ms until the server closes connections

device:connect.description: Authenticates and registers this connection as a device. MUST be the first message (ping excepted). Re-connecting with an existing deviceId supersedes the previous connection.

deviceId.description: MUST equal the token's device_id claim

protocolVersion.description: semver; major version must match the server's

device:connected.description: Confirms registration. protocolVersion is the negotiated version all further traffic follows.

device:disconnect.description: Explicitly unregisters this device (logout). Identity comes from the connection; no fields needed. The server confirms with device:disconnected, then the client may close the socket.

device:status.description: Sets this device's availability for receiving calls. Devices register as available by default.

call:initiate.description: Places a call to a business. callAttemptId is a client-generated UUIDv4 and must be globally unique (reuse => error duplicate_call_id). handle must match the token's business scope.

call:initiated.description: Acknowledges call:initiate: the call now exists and is ringing on devicesNotified devices.

call:incoming.audience: every available business device (WebSocket if connected; FCM data push for offline mobile devices). Also re-delivered to a business device that connects while the call is still ringing.
call:incoming.description: Notifies a business device of a ringing call.

call:cancel.description: Caller abandons the call before it is accepted. After acceptance use call:end.

call:cancelled.audience: reason=cancelled_by_caller: all ringing devices and the caller (as ack); reason=answered_elsewhere: every ringing device except the accepting one
call:cancelled.description: The ringing call no longer needs this device's attention.

call:accept.description: Accepts a ringing call. First accept wins; the sender must belong to the call's business. Identity comes from the connection.

mediaCapabilities.description: the acceptor's capabilities; assume audio-only if omitted

call:accepted.description: The call was accepted; the caller must now send webrtc:offer.

call:reject.description: Declines a ringing call on this device only. The call keeps ringing elsewhere; when every notified device has rejected, the caller receives call:unavailable (reason=all_devices_rejected). No per-reject message is sent to the caller.

call:unavailable.description: The call cannot be answered: no devices were available at initiate, or all devices rejected. Terminal.

call:busy.description: All otherwise-available devices are engaged in active calls. Terminal.

call:end.description: Either participant hangs up an accepted call. The server derives who ended it from the sender's role.

call:ended.audience: both participants (the ender receives it as ack)
call:ended.description: The call is over. duration is connected time in ms (0 if never connected).

call:failed.audience: s2c: both participants (or the caller, if the call had no callee yet)
call:failed.description: c2s: a participant reports an unrecoverable local failure (media permission denied, ICE failure after exhausting restarts). s2c: the server declares the call failed (relayed client report, reconnect grace expiry, or internal error). Terminal.

call:timeout.audience: phase=ringing: caller and all ringing devices; phase=connecting: both participants
call:timeout.description: A server timer expired before the call progressed. Terminal.

timeoutDuration.description: the timer's configured duration in ms

call:reconnect.description: After re-authenticating (device:connect) within reconnect_grace, a participant re-attaches to its in-flight call. The server re-binds signaling delivery to the new connection.

call:reconnected.description: Re-attachment succeeded; callState tells the client where the call currently stands.

webrtc:offer.description: SDP offer, relayed verbatim by the server. Initial negotiation: only the caller offers, after receiving call:accepted. Renegotiation: only the participant whose escalation/downgrade request was granted offers. This rule prevents glare.

webrtc:answer.description: SDP answer, relayed verbatim. Relaying the first answer moves the call to connected.

webrtc:ice-candidate.description: ICE candidate, relayed verbatim in either direction. Clients MUST buffer candidates that arrive before the remote description is set, and tolerate duplicates.

media:toggle.description: Informs the peer of a local media state change (mute, camera off, camera flip). Purely informational; does not change call state.

call:escalate.description: Requests voice-to-video escalation (voice calls only). The peer receives escalation:requested and must answer with escalation:accept or escalation:reject within escalation_timeout.

escalation:requested.description: The peer asked to upgrade the call to video.

escalation:accept.description: Accepts the pending escalation. The call's callType becomes video; the original requester then renegotiates via webrtc:offer.

escalation:reject.description: Declines the pending escalation. The call reverts to connected as a voice call.

escalation:accepted.description: Escalation granted; the call is now a video call. The requester must renegotiate via webrtc:offer.

escalation:rejected.description: Escalation declined or timed out; the call remains a voice call.

call:downgrade.description: Unilaterally downgrades a video call to voice (no peer consent required). Both participants receive call:downgraded; the requester then renegotiates via webrtc:offer.

call:downgraded.description: The call is now a voice call.

stateMachine.description: One server-authoritative state machine per call (keyed by callAttemptId). Clients never see states directly; they infer progress from messages. Each transition lists the event that triggers it.

initiated.on: call:incoming dispatched to at least one device
no available devices for the business
all available devices engaged in active calls

ringing.on: caller disconnected beyond reconnect_grace, or internal error

connecting.on: call:failed from a participant, or participant disconnected beyond reconnect_grace

connected.on: call:escalate from a participant (voice calls only)
call:failed from a participant, or participant disconnected beyond reconnect_grace

escalation_pending.on: call:failed from a participant, or participant disconnected beyond reconnect_grace
